steps.message:
<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `danger-full-access`: No filesystem sandboxing - all commands are permitted. Network access is enabled.
Approval policy is currently never. Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.
</permissions instructions><skills_instructions>
## Skills
A skill is a set of instructions provided through a `SKILL.md` source. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and source locator. `file` locators are on the host filesystem, `environment resource` locators are owned by an execution environment, `orchestrator resource` locators are opaque non-filesystem resources, and `custom resource` locators use their provider's access mechanism.
### Available skills
- imagegen: Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas. (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- openai-docs: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs, asks about Codex itself or choosing Codex surfaces, needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or model upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; use OpenAI docs MCP tools for non-Codex docs questions, use the Codex manual helper first for broad Codex self-knowledge, and restrict fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains. (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)
- plugin-creator: Create and scaffold plugin directories for Codex with a required `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, optional plugin folders/files, valid manifest defaults, and personal-marketplace entries by default. Use when Codex needs to create a new personal plugin, add optional plugin structure, generate or update marketplace entries for plugin ordering and availability metadata, or update an existing local plugin during development with the CLI-driven cachebuster and reinstall flow. (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/plugin-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /tmp/codex-home/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + source locator). `file` entries live on the host filesystem, `environment resource` entries are owned by their execution environment, `orchestrator resource` entries must be accessed through `skills.list` and `skills.read`, and `custom resource` entries use their provider's access mechanism.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or its source can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, the main agent must read its `SKILL.md` completely before taking task actions. For a `file` entry, open the listed path. For an `environment resource`, use the filesystem of the owning environment. For an `orchestrator resource`, call `skills.list` with `{"authority":{"kind":"orchestrator"}}`, select the matching package, and pass its `main_resource` to `skills.read`. If a read is truncated or paginated, continue until EOF.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references another resource, use the same access mechanism. Resolve relative paths against a filesystem-backed skill directory. For orchestrator skills, pass the exact referenced resource identifier with the same authority and package to `skills.read`; do not treat `skill://` identifiers as filesystem paths.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, use its routing instructions to identify the resources required for the task. The main agent must read each required instruction or reference file itself before acting on it. Do not delegate reading, summarizing, or interpreting skill instructions to a subagent. Subagents may still perform task work when the selected skill allows it.
  4) For filesystem-backed skills, prefer running or patching provided scripts instead of retyping large code blocks. For orchestrator skills, use `skills.read` and the available tools; do not invent a local path.
  5) Reuse provided assets or templates through the same source access mechanism instead of recreating them.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Progressive disclosure applies to selecting relevant files, not partially reading a selected instruction file. Do not load unrelated references, scripts, or assets.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

# Task description

The enable-wins flag (`EWFlag`) in `pkg/crdt/ewflag.go` violates its defining guarantee under concurrent updates. An EWFlag tracks a set of enable "dots" (per-node `(nodeID, counter)` observations); the flag reads as enabled whenever at least one enable dot is active. Disabling records the set of currently observed dots so a remove only clears what it has seen, while a concurrent enable—producing an unseen dot—survives. This is the enable-wins property.

The current `Merge` mishandles the case where the other replica concurrently disabled the flag and arrives with an empty dot set: merging clears a local enable dot that the other replica never observed, so a concurrent enable no longer wins over a concurrent disable. Symmetric ordering (merging in the other direction) and fully-converged states still behave correctly, which is why the defect only surfaces under a genuine concurrent enable/disable.

Fix `Merge` so that an enable dot is dropped only when it is causally dominated (observed and removed) by the other replica, never merely because the other replica carries no dots. Keep the existing exported method set, constructor, and value semantics (`Enabled() bool`) unchanged; the value must remain a deterministic function of the merged dot set, and convergence between any two replicas must hold regardless of merge order.

# Test guidelines

Run `go test ./pkg/crdt/...`. Add or extend table-driven tests in `pkg/crdt` covering: a concurrent enable on one replica versus a disable (empty dot set) on another, asserting the merged flag stays enabled in both merge directions; a disable that does observe an enable correctly clearing it; and repeated merges remaining idempotent and commutative.

# Lint guidelines

Run `gofmt -l pkg/crdt` and `go vet ./pkg/crdt/...`; both must report no issues before completion.

# Style guidelines

You are already on the correct starting snapshot. Create your branch from this state. Do not rebase or start from master, main, or any other branch.
I’ll inspect the CRDT implementation and tests first, then create a branch from the current snapshot before editing.

steps.message: The EWFlag already carries the causal context needed for the fix: `counters` records the highest enable dot each replica has observed, while `dots` is the currently active subset. I’m going to make `Merge` consult that context when deciding whether a missing dot was actually removed.

steps.message: I’m applying the merge fix and adding focused package tests. The main behavioral change is symmetric: a replica must also refuse to resurrect an incoming dot if its own causal context already observed and removed that exact dot.

steps.message: The merge logic is patched. I’m adding the regression tests now, including same-node concurrent enable after an observed old enable because that is the case where comparing causal counters matters most.

arguments.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /testbed/frontiercode-repo/pkg/crdt/ewflag_test.go
+package crdt
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"reflect"
+	"testing"
+)
+
+func TestEWFlagMergeEnableWinsConcurrentDisable(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name  string
+		setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "concurrent enable survives empty disable",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "concurrent enable from unseen node survives empty disable",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			left, right := tt.setup()
+
+			leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
+			if !leftFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge with disable first disabled a concurrent enable")
+			}
+
+			rightFirst := cloneEWFlag(right)
+			rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))
+			if !rightFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge with enable first failed to preserve concurrent enable")
+			}
+
+			assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestEWFlagMergeObservedDisableClearsEnable(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name  string
+		setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "observed disable clears active dot",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			left, right := tt.setup()
+
+			leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
+			if leftFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge kept an enable dot observed by a disable")
+			}
+
+			rightFirst := cloneEWFlag(right)
+			rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))
+			if rightFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge resurrected an enable dot observed by a disable")
+			}
+
+			assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestEWFlagMergeIdempotentAndCommutative(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name  string
+		setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "concurrent enable and disable",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "observed disable",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "independent concurrent enables",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				left := NewEWFlag()
+				right := NewEWFlag()
+
+				left.Enable("node-a")
+				right.Enable("node-b")
+
+				return left, right
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			left, right := tt.setup()
+
+			leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
+			mergedOnce := snapshotEWFlag(leftFirst)
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
+			if got := snapshotEWFlag(leftFirst); !reflect.DeepEqual(got, mergedOnce) {
+				t.Fatalf("merge is not idempotent: once=%v repeated=%v", mergedOnce, got)
+			}
+
+			rightFirst := cloneEWFlag(right)
+			rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))
+			assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func cloneEWFlag(flag *EWFlag) *EWFlag {
+	flag.mu.RLock()
+	defer flag.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	clone := NewEWFlag()
+	for nodeID, dot := range flag.dots {
+		clone.dots[nodeID] = dot
+	}
+	for nodeID, counter := range flag.counters {
+		clone.counters[nodeID] = counter
+	}
+	return clone
+}
+
+func assertSameEWFlagState(t *testing.T, left, right *EWFlag) {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	leftState := snapshotEWFlag(left)
+	rightState := snapshotEWFlag(right)
+	if !reflect.DeepEqual(leftState, rightState) {
+		t.Fatalf("merged states differ: left=%v right=%v", leftState, rightState)
+	}
+}
+
+func snapshotEWFlag(flag *EWFlag) map[string]string {
+	flag.mu.RLock()
+	defer flag.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	state := make(map[string]string, len(flag.dots)+len(flag.counters)+1)
+	state["value"] = fmt.Sprintf("%t", len(flag.dots) > 0)
+	for nodeID, dot := range flag.dots {
+		state["dot:"+nodeID] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", dot)
+	}
+	for nodeID, counter := range flag.counters {
+		state["counter:"+nodeID] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", counter)
+	}
+	return state
+}
*** End Patch

+package crdt
+
+import (

+)

+func TestEWFlagMergeEnableWinsConcurrentDisable(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name  string

+			if !rightFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge with enable first failed to preserve concurrent enable")
+			}
+
+			assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestEWFlagMergeObservedDisableClearsEnable(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name  string
+		setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "observed disable clears active dot",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			left, right := tt.setup()
+
+			leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
+			if leftFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge kept an enable dot observed by a disable")
+			}
+
+			rightFirst := cloneEWFlag(right)
+			rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))
+			if rightFirst.Value() {
+				t.Fatal("merge resurrected an enable dot observed by a disable")
+			}
+
+			assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestEWFlagMergeIdempotentAndCommutative(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name  string
+		setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "concurrent enable and disable",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "observed disable",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				enabled := NewEWFlag()
+				disabled := NewEWFlag()
+
+				enabled.Enable("node-a")
+				disabled.Merge(enabled)
+				disabled.Disable()
+
+				return enabled, disabled
+			},
+		},
+		{
+			name: "independent concurrent enables",
+			setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
+				left := NewEWFlag()
+				right := NewEWFlag()
+
+				left.Enable("node-a")
+				right.Enable("node-b")
+
+				return left, right
+			},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			left, right := tt.setup()
+
+			leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)

+			mergedOnce := snapshotEWFlag(leftFirst)
+			leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))

+			if got := snapshotEWFlag(leftFirst); !reflect.DeepEqual(got, mergedOnce) {
+				t.Fatalf("merge is not idempotent: once=%v repeated=%v", mergedOnce, got)
+			}
+

+			rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))
+			assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func cloneEWFlag(flag *EWFlag) *EWFlag {
+	flag.mu.RLock()
+	defer flag.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	clone := NewEWFlag()
+	for nodeID, dot := range flag.dots {
+		clone.dots[nodeID] = dot
+	}
+	for nodeID, counter := range flag.counters {
+		clone.counters[nodeID] = counter
+	}
+	return clone
+}
+
+func assertSameEWFlagState(t *testing.T, left, right *EWFlag) {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	leftState := snapshotEWFlag(left)
+	rightState := snapshotEWFlag(right)
+	if !reflect.DeepEqual(leftState, rightState) {
+		t.Fatalf("merged states differ: left=%v right=%v", leftState, rightState)
+	}
+}
+
+func snapshotEWFlag(flag *EWFlag) map[string]string {
+	flag.mu.RLock()
+	defer flag.mu.RUnlock()
+
+	state := make(map[string]string, len(flag.dots)+len(flag.counters)+1)
+	state["value"] = fmt.Sprintf("%t", len(flag.dots) > 0)
+	for nodeID, dot := range flag.dots {
+		state["dot:"+nodeID] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", dot)
+	}
+	for nodeID, counter := range flag.counters {
+		state["counter:"+nodeID] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", counter)
+	}
+	return state
+}

     9	func TestEWFlagMergeEnableWinsConcurrentDisable(t *testing.T) {
    10		tests := []struct {
    11			name  string
    12			setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
    13		}{
    14			{
    15				name: "concurrent enable survives empty disable",
    16				setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
    17					enabled := NewEWFlag()
    18					disabled := NewEWFlag()
    19	
    20					enabled.Enable("node-a")
    21					disabled.Merge(enabled)
    22	
    23					enabled.Enable("node-a")
    24					disabled.Disable()
    25	
    26					return enabled, disabled
    27				},
    28			},
    29			{
    30				name: "concurrent enable from unseen node survives empty disable",
    31				setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
    32					enabled := NewEWFlag()
    33					disabled := NewEWFlag()
    34	
    35					enabled.Enable("node-a")
    36					disabled.Disable()
    37	
    38					return enabled, disabled
    39				},
    40			},

    45				left, right := tt.setup()
    46	
    47				leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)
    48				leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
    49				if !leftFirst.Value() {
    50					t.Fatal("merge with disable first disabled a concurrent enable")
    51				}
    52	
    53				rightFirst := cloneEWFlag(right)
    54				rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))

    56					t.Fatal("merge with enable first failed to preserve concurrent enable")
    57				}

    60			})

   105	func TestEWFlagMergeIdempotentAndCommutative(t *testing.T) {
   106		tests := []struct {
   107			name  string
   108			setup func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag)
   109		}{
   110			{
   111				name: "concurrent enable and disable",
   112				setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
   113					enabled := NewEWFlag()
   114					disabled := NewEWFlag()
   115	
   116					enabled.Enable("node-a")
   117					disabled.Merge(enabled)
   118					enabled.Enable("node-a")
   119					disabled.Disable()
   120	
   121					return enabled, disabled
   122				},
   123			},
   124			{
   125				name: "observed disable",
   126				setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
   127					enabled := NewEWFlag()
   128					disabled := NewEWFlag()
   129	
   130					enabled.Enable("node-a")
   131					disabled.Merge(enabled)
   132					disabled.Disable()
   133	
   134					return enabled, disabled
   135				},
   136			},
   137			{
   138				name: "independent concurrent enables",
   139				setup: func() (*EWFlag, *EWFlag) {
   140					left := NewEWFlag()
   141					right := NewEWFlag()
   142	
   143					left.Enable("node-a")
   144					right.Enable("node-b")
   145	
   146					return left, right
   147				},
   148			},
   149		}
   150	
   151		for _, tt := range tests {
   152			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
   153				left, right := tt.setup()
   154	
   155				leftFirst := cloneEWFlag(left)
   156				leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
   157				mergedOnce := snapshotEWFlag(leftFirst)
   158				leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
   159				leftFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(right))
   160				if got := snapshotEWFlag(leftFirst); !reflect.DeepEqual(got, mergedOnce) {
   161					t.Fatalf("merge is not idempotent: once=%v repeated=%v", mergedOnce, got)
   162				}
   163	
   164				rightFirst := cloneEWFlag(right)
   165				rightFirst.Merge(cloneEWFlag(left))
   166				assertSameEWFlagState(t, leftFirst, rightFirst)
   167			})
   168		}
   169	}
   170	
   171	func cloneEWFlag(flag *EWFlag) *EWFlag {
   172		flag.mu.RLock()
   173		defer flag.mu.RUnlock()
   174	
   175		clone := NewEWFlag()
   176		for nodeID, dot := range flag.dots {
   177			clone.dots[nodeID] = dot
   178		}
   179		for nodeID, counter := range flag.counters {
   180			clone.counters[nodeID] = counter
   181		}
   182		return clone
   183	}
   184	
   185	func assertSameEWFlagState(t *testing.T, left, right *EWFlag) {
   186		t.Helper()
   187	
   188		leftState := snapshotEWFlag(left)
   189		rightState := snapshotEWFlag(right)
   190		if !reflect.DeepEqual(leftState, rightState) {
   191			t.Fatalf("merged states differ: left=%v right=%v", leftState, rightState)
   192		}
   193	}
   194	
   195	func snapshotEWFlag(flag *EWFlag) map[string]string {
   196		flag.mu.RLock()
   197		defer flag.mu.RUnlock()
   198	
   199		state := make(map[string]string, len(flag.dots)+len(flag.counters)+1)
   200		state["value"] = fmt.Sprintf("%t", len(flag.dots) > 0)
   201		for nodeID, dot := range flag.dots {
   202			state["dot:"+nodeID] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", dot)
   203		}
   204		for nodeID, counter := range flag.counters {
   205			state["counter:"+nodeID] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", counter)
   206		}
   207		return state
   208	}

steps.message:
Implemented the EWFlag merge fix in [pkg/crdt/ewflag.go](/testbed/frontiercode-repo/pkg/crdt/ewflag.go:45). `Merge` now drops or refuses an enable dot only when the peer/local causal counter proves that exact dot was observed and removed, so an empty dot set alone no longer clears concurrent enables.

Added table-driven regression coverage in [pkg/crdt/ewflag_test.go](/testbed/frontiercode-repo/pkg/crdt/ewflag_test.go:9) for concurrent enable vs empty disable in both merge directions, observed disable clearing without resurrection, and repeated idempotent/commutative merges.

I could not create a branch because `/testbed/frontiercode-repo` has no `.git` directory. I also could not run the requested checks because this environment has neither `go` nor `gofmt` installed:
`gofmt -l pkg/crdt`, `go test ./pkg/crdt/...`, and `go vet ./pkg/crdt/...` all fail with command-not-found.
